japan.title: Japan Student Visa & Scholarship Guide 2026 for international Students
japan.description: A complete roadmap for international students seeking higher education in Japan. Covering the COE process, MEXT and JASSO scholarship details, comprehensive English proficiency requirements (IELTS, PTE, Duolingo), financial solvency, and 2026 approval trends.

visa_category_details.entry_type: Single Entry (Converted to Residence Card upon arrival)
visa_category_details.eligibility: Applicants with a valid Admission Offer and an issued Certificate of Eligibility (COE) from the Ministry of Justice, Japan.

government_funded.name: MEXT (Monbukagakusho) Scholarship
government_funded.benefits: Full tuition waiver, monthly stipend (¥117,000 - ¥145,000), and round-trip airfare.
government_funded.application_window: April–May (Embassy Recommendation) or via University Recommendation.
government_funded.selection_criteria: High academic GPA, research proposal (for postgrads), and written exams.

private_and_partial.name: JASSO Reservation Program
private_and_partial.benefits: Monthly stipend of ¥48,000 for EJU high-scorers.
private_and_partial.target: Self-funded students with excellent entrance results.

private_and_partial.name: Honjo International Scholarship Foundation
private_and_partial.benefits: Monthly stipend up to ¥210,000 for graduate students.
private_and_partial.special_note: Requires proficiency in Japanese or high-level English research.

private_and_partial.name: Maki Scholarship Foundation
private_and_partial.benefits: Covers tuition and living costs for specific partner universities in Japan.
private_and_partial.target: Undergraduate students from Asian countries.

english_language_test_requirements_2026.overview: For English-Medium Programs (G30/Global Programs), standardized test scores are mandatory. Requirements vary by university rank.

duolingo_det.acceptance_note: Widely accepted by private universities and some public research institutions post-2024.

toefl_ibt.note: Preferred by top-tier National Universities (UTokyo, Kyoto, Osaka).

financial_solvency_thresholds.self_funded_min_balance: BDT 2,500,000 - 3,500,000 (Equivalent to 1st year tuition + ¥2,000,000 living costs)
financial_solvency_thresholds.sponsor_profile: First-degree relative (Father/Mother) preferred. Sponsors must show stable monthly income and professional status.
financial_solvency_thresholds.bank_history_requirement: 6 to 12 months of consistent transaction history. Sudden 'lump-sum' deposits without source-of-fund proof lead to rejection.
financial_solvency_thresholds.logic: The Japanese Immigration Bureau requires proof that the student can survive without illegal work and cover all educational expenses.

embassy_submission_docs: Original Passport (Valid for duration of stay)
Original COE (Certificate of Eligibility) + 1 Photocopy
Visa Application Form (Typed with QR Code)
One Photo (45mm x 45mm, White background, Matte finish)
Acceptance Letter from the Japanese Institution
Educational Certificates & Transcripts (Attested by Ministry of Education & MOFA Bangladesh)
coe_phase_documents: Financial Support Declaration Form (Signed by Sponsor)
Sponsor's Bank Solvency & 6-month Detailed Statement
Sponsor's Income Tax Return (ITDR) for last 3 years
Trade License or Job Certificate (NOC) of the Sponsor
Relationship Proof (Birth Certificate or Family Tree from Ward Counselor/Union Parishad)
Language Proficiency Proof (JLPT N5+ / NAT-Test Q5 for language students; IELTS/PTE/Duolingo for English programs)

processing_and_approval_metrics.coe_processing_time: 2 - 3 months (Issued by regional Immigration Bureaus like Tokyo, Osaka, Nagoya)
processing_and_approval_metrics.visa_processing_time: 5 - 10 working days after COE submission at JVAC (Japan Visa Application Centre)
processing_and_approval_metrics.approval_chances: High (95%+) once a COE is issued, provided data matches perfectly with original submission.
processing_and_approval_metrics.interview_probability: Medium. Specifically for language school students to verify basic Hiragana/Katakana and 'Study Motivation'.

high_risk_reasons: Inconsistency between documents submitted to School vs documents submitted to Embassy.
Inability to explain the 'Study Gap' logically.
Inadequate Japanese language ability for language school tracks.
Suspicious financial documents (Fake bank statements or unverifiable income sources).
Previous visa rejection from any country (must be disclosed).
rejection_risk_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Ensure all translations (Bengali to English/Japanese) are notarized by authorized translators and information remains 100% consistent across all phases.

work_rights_and_stay.part_time_permission: Up to 28 hours per week (Requires 'Shikakugai Katsu' sticker at airport immigration).
work_rights_and_stay.vacation_hours: Up to 40 hours per week during official semester breaks (Spring/Summer/Winter).
work_rights_and_stay.visa_extension: Renewable based on attendance (must stay above 80-90%) and academic performance.
work_rights_and_stay.post_study_work: Eligible to switch to a 'Humanities/Engineer' work visa upon securing a job offer (minimum salary approx. ¥200,000/month).

step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_1: Research Universities/Language Schools and check English (IELTS/PTE) or Japanese (JLPT) requirements.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_2: Submit application to the institution and pass the interview.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_3: Pay the screening fee and submit financial documents for the COE application.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_4: Wait for COE (Issued by Japanese Immigration).
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_5: Pay 1st-semester tuition fees to the institution and receive original COE.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_6: Submit Visa application at JVAC Bangladesh with the original COE.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_7: Fly to Japan and receive the Residence Card (Zairyu Card) at the airport.

faq_student_edition.question: Can I apply with Duolingo for 2026 intake?
faq_student_edition.answer: Yes, many private universities and language institutes in Japan now accept Duolingo (DET) as a cost-effective alternative to IELTS, typically requiring a score of 105+.

faq_student_edition.question: Is a study gap acceptable for Japan?
faq_student_edition.answer: Gaps of 2-5 years are generally accepted with a valid explanation (work experience or skill development). Gaps over 5 years require much stronger documentation of motivation.

faq_student_edition.question: What is the 'Permission to Engage in Activity other than that Permitted'?
faq_student_edition.answer: It is the legal authorization for students to work part-time. You should apply for this immediately upon landing at the Japanese airport to avoid delays.

canada.title: Canada Student Visa & Scholarship Guides for international Students
canada.description: A complete roadmap for international students seeking higher education in Canada. Covering the PAL and Study Permit process, Entrance and Government scholarship details, comprehensive English proficiency requirements (IELTS, PTE, Duolingo), financial solvency (GIC), and 2026 approval trends.

visa_category_details.entry_type: TRV or eTA (Issued alongside Study Permit approval)
visa_category_details.eligibility: Applicants with a valid Letter of Acceptance (LOA) and a Provincial Attestation Letter (PAL) from a Designated Learning Institution (DLI).

government_funded.name: Vanier Canada Graduate Scholarships
government_funded.benefits: $50,000 per year for three years for high-quality doctoral students.
government_funded.selection_criteria: Academic excellence, research potential, and leadership skills.

private_and_partial.name: Lester B. Pearson International Scholarships
private_and_partial.benefits: Covers tuition, books, incidental fees, and full residence support for 4 years.
private_and_partial.target: Exceptional undergraduate students at the University of Toronto.

private_and_partial.name: Karen McKellin International Leader of Tomorrow Award
private_and_partial.benefits: Need-based funding covering tuition and living costs.
private_and_partial.special_note: Awarded by UBC to international undergraduates with superior academic records.

private_and_partial.name: Pierre Elliott Trudeau Foundation Scholarships
private_and_partial.benefits: Up to $60,000 per year for three years for doctoral candidates in humanities/social sciences.
private_and_partial.target: Social science PhD students with a focus on public issues.

english_language_test_requirements_2026.overview: For English-Medium programs, standardized test scores are mandatory. SDS (Student Direct Stream) equivalents often require higher sub-scores.

duolingo_det.acceptance_note: Widely accepted by Colleges and many Universities for non-SDS applications.

toefl_ibt.note: Preferred by top-tier research universities (UofT, McGill, UBC).

financial_solvency_thresholds.self_funded_min_balance: BDT 3,000,000 - 4,500,000 (Equivalent to 1st year tuition + GIC of $20,635 CAD)
financial_solvency_thresholds.sponsor_profile: First-degree relative (Father/Mother). Sponsors must show significant liquid assets and stable business/employment ties.
financial_solvency_thresholds.bank_history_requirement: 4 to 6 months of consistent transaction history. Proof of GIC (Guaranteed Investment Certificate) is now the primary financial benchmark.
financial_solvency_thresholds.logic: IRCC requires proof that the student has sufficient funds to cover tuition and the updated cost-of-living requirement without working.

embassy_submission_docs: Original Passport (Valid for the duration of intended stay)
Study Permit Approval Letter (Correspondence Letter)
Visa Application Forms (IMM 1294, 5645, 5257)
Two Photos (35mm x 45mm, White background, Matte finish)
Letter of Acceptance (LOA) and Provincial Attestation Letter (PAL)
Educational Certificates & Transcripts (Notarized and attested)
coe_phase_documents: Financial Support Affidavit (Notarized)
GIC (Guaranteed Investment Certificate) Confirmation
Sponsor's Income Tax Returns (Last 3 years)
Property Valuation and Asset Proof
Relationship Proof (Birth Certificate/National ID)
Language Proficiency Proof (IELTS/PTE/Duolingo scores)

processing_and_approval_metrics.coe_processing_time: 1 - 2 months (Time to secure LOA and Provincial Attestation Letter)
processing_and_approval_metrics.visa_processing_time: 8 - 12 weeks for Bangladesh (Standard processing through VFS Global)
processing_and_approval_metrics.approval_chances: Moderate to High (Depends heavily on SOP, PAL availability, and financial clarity).
processing_and_approval_metrics.interview_probability: Low. IRCC usually decides based on the paper/online application but may call for an interview in rare cases.

high_risk_reasons: Weak Statement of Purpose (SOP) failing to prove 'Dual Intent'.
Inadequate financial proof or lack of GIC for non-SDS streams.
Program 'Mismatch' relative to previous academic or work history.
Undisclosed previous visa rejections (Misrepresentation).
Insufficient 'Home Ties' indicating the student will not leave Canada after study.
rejection_risk_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Draft a highly specific SOP that links the Canadian degree to a concrete career path in Bangladesh and ensure all financial documents are verifiable.

work_rights_and_stay.part_time_permission: Up to 24 hours per week (Updated from 20 hours in 2024/25).
work_rights_and_stay.vacation_hours: Full-time work allowed during scheduled academic breaks (Summer/Winter).
work_rights_and_stay.visa_extension: Study Permit extension must be applied for 30 days before the current permit expires.
work_rights_and_stay.post_study_work: Eligible for PGWP (Post-Graduation Work Permit) for up to 3 years depending on the program length.

step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_1: Research DLIs and ensure the program is eligible for PGWP and receive an LOA.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_2: Apply for and receive the Provincial Attestation Letter (PAL) from the university/province.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_3: Purchase a GIC ($20,635+ CAD) from a participating Canadian bank (Scotiabank, CIBC, etc.).
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_4: Upfront Medical Exam at an IRCC-approved panel physician.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_5: Submit the Study Permit application online via the IRCC portal.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_6: Give Biometrics at VFS Global Dhaka/Chittagong and wait for eligibility review.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_7: Receive Passport Request (PPR), get visa stamped, and receive Study Permit at the Port of Entry.

faq_student_edition.question: Can I apply with Duolingo for 2026 intake?
faq_student_edition.answer: Yes, many Canadian colleges and several universities accept Duolingo for admission. However, for visa purposes, ensure your overall profile remains strong as it is a non-SDS application.

faq_student_edition.question: Is a study gap acceptable for Canada?
faq_student_edition.answer: Gaps of 2-8 years are common for Master’s applicants if backed by work experience. Undergraduate gaps over 2 years require a strong explanation in the SOP.

faq_student_edition.question: What is the 'Provincial Attestation Letter' (PAL)?
faq_student_edition.answer: It is a mandatory document introduced in 2024 that verifies the student is within the provincial cap for international study permits. You cannot apply for a visa without it.

australia.title: Australia Student Visa & Scholarship Guide 2026 for international Students
australia.description: A complete roadmap for international students seeking higher education in Australia. Covering the CoE and Genuine Student (GS) process, Australia Awards and Destination Australia scholarships, English requirements (IELTS, PTE), financial capacity, and 2026 approval metrics.

visa_category_details.entry_type: Multiple Entry (Valid for the duration of the course)
visa_category_details.eligibility: Applicants with a valid Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE) and who satisfy the 'Genuine Student' (GS) requirement.

government_funded.name: Australia Awards Scholarships
government_funded.benefits: Full tuition, return air travel, establishment allowance, and Contribution to Living Expenses (CLE).
government_funded.application_window: February–April annually (for the following year's intake).
government_funded.selection_criteria: Academic merit, leadership potential, and contribution to Bangladesh's development.

private_and_partial.name: Destination Australia Scholarship
private_and_partial.benefits: Up to AUD 15,000 per year for students studying in regional Australia.
private_and_partial.target: Students enrolled at regional campus locations (e.g., Adelaide, Perth, Hobart).

private_and_partial.name: Vice-Chancellor's International Scholarships
private_and_partial.benefits: Range from AUD 10,000 to 50% tuition fee reduction.
private_and_partial.special_note: Offered by universities like USyd, Monash, and Deakin for high-achieving students.

private_and_partial.name: Research Training Program (RTP)
private_and_partial.benefits: Tuition fee offset and a living stipend of approx. AUD 32,000 - 35,000 annually.
private_and_partial.target: Masters by Research or PhD candidates.

english_language_test_requirements_2026.overview: Australia strictly requires English proficiency for Subclass 500. While universities might accept others, the Department of Home Affairs has specific accepted tests.

duolingo_det.acceptance_note: Accepted by some universities for admission, but usually NOT accepted by Home Affairs for the visa unless an alternative is provided.

toefl_ibt.note: Widely accepted; scores must be from a single sitting.

financial_solvency_thresholds.self_funded_min_balance: BDT 4,500,000 - 6,000,000 (Equivalent to 1st year tuition + AUD 29,710 living costs + travel).
financial_solvency_thresholds.sponsor_profile: Parents, spouse, or close relatives. Must show an annual income of at least AUD 87,856 if no secondary applicants are included.
financial_solvency_thresholds.bank_history_requirement: 3 to 6 months of stable history or a sanctioned Education Loan from a Grade-A bank. Sudden deposits require proof of asset sale/income.
financial_solvency_thresholds.logic: The 'Genuine Access' rule ensures that the funds aren't just for show but are actually available for the student's use in Australia.

embassy_submission_docs: Valid Passport
Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE) - All codes if packaged
Overseas Student Health Cover (OSHC) for the full visa duration
Digital Photo (35mm x 45mm, White background)
Genuine Student (GS) Statement (Detailed answers to specific logic questions)
Academic Documents (Transcripts/Certificates) - Scanned originals
coe_phase_documents: Financial Evidence (Bank Statements / Loan Letters)
Proof of Relationship with Sponsor (Birth Cert / Marriage Cert)
Sponsor’s Employment/Business Proof (Trade License / Tax Returns)
Statement of Purpose (SOP) for University admission
English Test Report Form (IELTS/PTE)
HAP ID for Medical Examination

processing_and_approval_metrics.visa_processing_time: 15 - 45 days (80% of applications are processed within this window)
processing_and_approval_metrics.approval_chances: High for Level-1 Universities; Moderate for VET/Private Colleges. Focus is on 'Genuine Student' intent.
processing_and_approval_metrics.interview_probability: Low to Medium. Conducted by the High Commission to verify the GS statement claims.

high_risk_reasons: Failure to explain why an Australian degree is better than a Bangladeshi degree (ROI).
Applying for a course lower than current qualification level (AQF downgrade).
Unexplained study gaps exceeding 1-2 years.
Inadequate funds or inability to prove the source of sponsor's wealth.
Poor performance in the GS interview if called.
rejection_risk_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Provide a detailed GS response that links the specific modules of the Australian course to high-paying job opportunities in Bangladesh.

work_rights_and_stay.part_time_permission: Up to 48 hours per fortnight (24 hours per week average).
work_rights_and_stay.vacation_hours: Unlimited working hours during official university holiday periods.
work_rights_and_stay.visa_extension: Possible to renew onshore if continuing to a higher level course (e.g., Bachelor to Master).
work_rights_and_stay.post_study_work: Eligible for Subclass 485 (Temporary Graduate Visa) for 2–4 years depending on the degree and location (Regional bonus).

step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_1: Apply to a DLI (Designated Learning Institution) and clear the university's internal GS/GTE interview.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_3: Pay the 1st semester tuition fee and OSHC (Health Insurance) to receive the CoE.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_4: Generate HAP ID and complete the medical check-up at an approved clinic.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_5: Prepare the Genuine Student (GS) responses and upload all financial/academic docs to ImmiAccount.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_6: Lodge the Subclass 500 visa application and pay the visa fee (approx. AUD 1,600 - 2,000).
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_7: Provide biometrics at VFS Global and wait for the Grant Letter.

faq_student_edition.question: Can I use Duolingo for an Australia Visa in 2026?
faq_student_edition.answer: While some universities accept Duolingo for admission, the Australian Department of Home Affairs generally requires IELTS or PTE for the visa application itself.

faq_student_edition.question: What is the 'Genuine Student' (GS) requirement?
faq_student_edition.answer: Effective 2024/25, the GS replaces the old GTE. It involves answering specific questions in the application about your study intent and career value.

faq_student_edition.question: Do I need to pay the full year's tuition before applying?
faq_student_edition.answer: No, you usually only need to pay the first semester's tuition fee (as stated in your Offer Letter) to get your CoE for the visa application.

european-union.title: Europe (Schengen) Student Visa & Scholarship Guide for international Students
european-union.description: A complete roadmap for international students seeking higher education in the EU. Covering the Blocked Account (Germany) vs. Financial Guarantee (France) processes, Erasmus Mundus scholarships, English proficiency (IELTS, PTE, Duolingo), and 2026 Schengen visa protocols.

visa_category_details.entry_type: Multi-Entry (Allows travel across the Schengen Area for 90 days in 180 days)
visa_category_details.eligibility: Applicants with a valid University Admission Letter (Zulassungsbescheid/EEF) and proof of financial subsistence for the duration of study.

government_funded.name: Erasmus Mundus Joint Masters (EMJM)
government_funded.benefits: Full tuition waiver, monthly stipend of €1,400, travel costs, and comprehensive insurance.
government_funded.selection_criteria: Excellence-based; requires a top-tier Bachelor's degree and strong motivation letter.

private_and_partial.name: DAAD Scholarships (Germany)
private_and_partial.benefits: Monthly stipend (€934 - €1,200), health insurance, and travel allowance.
private_and_partial.target: Graduate students with at least two years of professional experience.

private_and_partial.name: Eiffel Excellence Scholarship (France)
private_and_partial.benefits: Monthly allowance of €1,181 - €1,800, return trip, and social security.
private_and_partial.special_note: Applications must be submitted by the French institution on behalf of the student.

private_and_partial.name: Holland Scholarship (Netherlands)
private_and_partial.benefits: One-time payment of €5,000 to €15,000 for the first year of study.
private_and_partial.target: International students from outside the EEA.

english_language_test_requirements_2026.overview: While many public universities in Europe are free, English-taught programs require high scores. Requirements are strict for Tier-1 research universities.

duolingo_det.acceptance_note: Widely accepted in Ireland, Italy, and private German universities; less common in public German TU9s.

toefl_ibt.note: Standard for programs in Scandinavia and the Netherlands.

financial_solvency_thresholds.self_funded_min_balance: BDT 1,500,000 - 2,500,000 (Varies by country; Germany requires a Blocked Account).
financial_solvency_thresholds.sponsor_profile: Immediate family or third-party sponsors with a 'Formal Obligation' (Verpflichtungserklärung).
financial_solvency_thresholds.bank_history_requirement: Germany: Blocked Account of €11,208. France: Proof of approx. €615/month. Minimum 3–6 months history required for other EU states.
financial_solvency_thresholds.logic: Schengen states must ensure you have funds to cover the 'Socio-Cultural Minimum' without relying on social benefits.

embassy_submission_docs: Valid Passport (issued within 10 years, 3+ months validity)
Schengen Visa Form (National Type D for long stay)
University Admission Letter (Original + Copies)
Blocked Account Confirmation (Germany) or Solvency Certificate
Schengen Travel Insurance (min. €30,000 coverage)
Proof of Accommodation (for first 3 months)
coe_phase_documents: Motivation Letter (Statement of Purpose)
Curriculum Vitae (Europass Format)
Attested Academic Certificates (MOFA & Education Board)
Language Proficiency Certificate (IELTS/PTE/German A1-C1)
Birth Certificate (English translated and notarized)
Visa Fee Receipt (approx. €75 - €100)

processing_and_approval_metrics.coe_processing_time: 1 - 3 months (Admission assessment and VFS/Embassy appointment wait times)
processing_and_approval_metrics.visa_processing_time: 15 days to 2 months (Long-stay visas require local authority approval in the host city)
processing_and_approval_metrics.approval_chances: High for public university admits; Moderate for private or language-only tracks.
processing_and_approval_metrics.interview_probability: High. Mandatory interview at the Embassy or VFS to test language and academic motivation.

high_risk_reasons: Inability to explain why a specific German/French/Italian city was chosen.
Significant study gaps without professional documentation.
Lack of progress in German/French language if applying for taught programs.
Invalid Travel Insurance that doesn't meet Schengen Article 15 standards.
Inconsistent travel history or previous Schengen rejections.
rejection_risk_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Use the Europass CV format and ensure your SOP explains exactly how the European degree benefits your career back in Bangladesh.

work_rights_and_stay.part_time_permission: 140 full days or 280 half days per year (approx. 20 hours per week).
work_rights_and_stay.vacation_hours: Full-time work allowed during semester breaks (Lecture-free periods).
work_rights_and_stay.visa_extension: Renewable yearly as a 'Residence Permit' (Aufenthaltstitel) at the local Foreigners' Office (Ausländerbehörde).
work_rights_and_stay.post_study_work: Eligible for an 18-month Job Seeker Visa (Germany) or 12-month APS (France) after graduation.

step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_1: Apply to public or private universities via Uni-Assist (Germany) or Campus France.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_2: Receive Admission Letter and open a Blocked Account (e.g., via Fintiba or Expatrio).
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_3: Book a Visa Appointment via VFS Global or the National Embassy/Consulate.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_4: Attest all academic documents from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MOFA) Bangladesh.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_5: Attend the Visa Interview and submit biometric data.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_6: Receive the stamped Visa and fly to Europe.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_7: Register your address (Anmeldung) and apply for the Residence Permit within 90 days.

faq_student_edition.question: Can I apply for 2026 intake with Duolingo?
faq_student_edition.answer: Yes for Ireland and many private schools in Italy/Germany, but most German public universities still strictly require IELTS 6.5 or TOEFL 90+.

faq_student_edition.question: Are tuition fees really free in Europe?
faq_student_edition.answer: In German public universities, tuition is free (only a small semester fee), while in France/Italy, fees are significantly lower than in the UK/USA for public institutions.

faq_student_edition.question: Do I need to speak the local language?
faq_student_edition.answer: For English-taught programs, you don't 'need' it for the visa, but basic knowledge (A1/A2) is highly recommended to show 'Genuine Integration' intent.

united-states.title: United States America (USA) Student Visa & Scholarship Guide for international Students
united-states.description: A complete roadmap for international students seeking higher education in the United States. Covering the I-20 and SEVIS process, F-1 Visa Interview strategies, comprehensive English proficiency requirements (IELTS, TOEFL, Duolingo), and 2026 financial solvency benchmarks.

visa_category_details.entry_type: Multiple Entry (Valid for the duration of the I-20 status)
purpose: English as a Second Language (ESL) Programs
visa_category_details.eligibility: Applicants must be accepted by an SEVP-certified institution and receive an 'I-20 Certificate of Eligibility'.

government_funded.name: Fulbright Foreign Student Program
government_funded.benefits: Full tuition, living stipend, airfare, and health insurance for graduate students.
government_funded.selection_criteria: Academic excellence, leadership potential, and commitment to cross-cultural exchange.

private_and_partial.name: Institutional Merit-Based Scholarships
private_and_partial.benefits: Varies from $5,000 to full-tuition waivers.
private_and_partial.target: International applicants with high SAT/ACT or GRE/GMAT scores.

private_and_partial.name: Graduate Assistantships (TA/RA)
private_and_partial.benefits: Full tuition coverage plus a monthly stipend in exchange for teaching or research work.
private_and_partial.special_note: Primarily available for Master's and PhD candidates at research-intensive universities.

private_and_partial.name: #YouAreWelcomeHere Scholarship
private_and_partial.benefits: Renewable tuition scholarships of at least 50% for undergraduate students.
private_and_partial.target: International students dedicated to promoting intercultural learning.

english_language_test_requirements_2026.overview: For US universities, English proficiency is a standard admission requirement. While many accept Duolingo, top-tier institutions prefer TOEFL or IELTS.

duolingo_det.acceptance_note: Accepted by over 4,500 US institutions, including several Ivy League schools, as of 2026.

toefl_ibt.note: The gold standard for US universities; scores must be sent directly via ETS.

financial_solvency_thresholds.self_funded_min_balance: BDT 5,500,000 - 8,000,000 (Equivalent to 1st year tuition + living expenses as listed on the I-20).
financial_solvency_thresholds.sponsor_profile: Parents are preferred sponsors. Must show liquid funds, stable professional income, and asset ownership (Land/Property).
financial_solvency_thresholds.bank_history_requirement: 6 months of consistent bank statements. Significant 'unexplained' large deposits just before the interview are highly discouraged.
financial_solvency_thresholds.logic: Under Section 214(b), you must prove you can afford the expensive US education without resorting to unauthorized off-campus work.

embassy_submission_docs: Original Passport (Valid for at least 6 months)
Original I-20 Form (Signed by both the student and DSO)
DS-160 Confirmation Page (with barcode)
SEVIS I-901 Fee Receipt ($350 for F-1)
Visa Interview Appointment Confirmation
One Photo (51mm x 51mm, White background, taken within 6 months)
coe_phase_documents: Affidavit of Support from Sponsor
Sponsor's Bank Solvency & Detailed 6-month Transaction History
Sponsor's Income Tax Returns (ITDR) for last 3 years
Notarized Property Valuation & Asset Documents
Educational Certificates (SSC/HSC/Bachelor) & Original Transcripts
Standardized Test Scores (SAT/GRE/GMAT and IELTS/TOEFL)

processing_and_approval_metrics.coe_processing_time: 1 - 3 weeks (Time for university to issue I-20 after admission and financial verification)
processing_and_approval_metrics.visa_processing_time: Immediate (Result usually given at the end of the 2–3 minute interview; passport returned in 3-5 days).
processing_and_approval_metrics.approval_chances: Varies. High for those with strong academics and clear home ties; strict for weak SOP/financial cases.
processing_and_approval_metrics.interview_probability: 100% (Mandatory). Face-to-face interview with a Consular Officer is the most critical part of the process.

high_risk_reasons: Failure to demonstrate 'Strong Ties' to Bangladesh (Section 214(b) - Potential Immigrant).
Robotic or memorized answers during the interview.
Inability to clearly explain why you chose that specific university or program.
Discrepancies between DS-160 data and verbal interview statements.
Inadequate funding for subsequent years of study beyond the first year.
rejection_risk_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Focus on the 'Why US' and 'Why this major' answers. Be conversational and confident; show that you are a genuine student who will return home.

work_rights_and_stay.part_time_permission: Up to 20 hours per week (On-campus only during the first academic year).
work_rights_and_stay.vacation_hours: Full-time on-campus work allowed during summer and winter breaks.
work_rights_and_stay.visa_extension: Renewable based on academic progress (requires a new I-20 for program extensions).
work_rights_and_stay.post_study_work: Eligible for OPT (Optional Practical Training) for 12 months, plus a 24-month extension for STEM majors (3 years total).

step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_1: Apply to SEVP-certified US universities and secure your Letter of Admission.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_2: Submit financial documents to the university to receive your official Form I-20.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_3: Pay the SEVIS I-901 fee online and keep the payment receipt.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_4: Complete the DS-160 Online Visa Application and pay the $185 visa fee.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_5: Schedule two appointments: Biometrics at the VAC and the Visa Interview at the Embassy.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_6: Attend the interview at the US Embassy in Dhaka and receive a verbal approval/denial.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_7: Prepare to fly to the US no more than 30 days before the start date on your I-20.

faq_student_edition.question: Can I apply with Duolingo for 2026 intake?
faq_student_edition.answer: Yes, many reputable US universities accept Duolingo (DET) for admission. However, ensure you also prepare for the visa interview where your spoken English will be evaluated.

faq_student_edition.question: Is a study gap acceptable for the USA?
faq_student_edition.answer: Study gaps of 2-5 years are generally accepted if you have relevant work experience. You must be prepared to explain the gap clearly to the Consular Officer.

faq_student_edition.question: What is the 214(b) rejection?
faq_student_edition.answer: It is the most common rejection reason, meaning you haven't sufficiently proven that you have strong ties to your home country and intend to return after graduation.

albania.title: Albania Student Visa & Residency Guide for International Students
albania.description: A complete roadmap for international students seeking higher education in Albania. Covering the Type D/ST Visa and Residence Permit process, government and university scholarships, language requirements (English/Albanian), financial capacity, and 2026 approval metrics.

visa_category_details.entry_type: Single/Multiple Entry (Converted to Residence Permit upon arrival)
visa_category_details.eligibility: Applicants with a Letter of Acceptance from an accredited Albanian HEI (Higher Education Institution) and proof of tuition payment.

government_funded.name: Ministry of Education and Sports Scholarships
government_funded.benefits: Full or partial tuition waivers and monthly stipends for specific bilateral partner countries.
government_funded.selection_criteria: Academic excellence, reciprocity agreements, and field of study relevance.

private_and_partial.name: Erasmus+ Key Action 1 (International Credit Mobility)
private_and_partial.benefits: Monthly stipend (approx. €800) and travel cost coverage for exchange semesters.
private_and_partial.target: Students from partner universities outside the EU studying in Tirana or Durrës.

private_and_partial.name: University Merit Discounts
private_and_partial.benefits: Range from 10% to 50% tuition fee reduction.
private_and_partial.special_note: Offered by private institutions like University of New York Tirana (UNYT) or Epoka University.

private_and_partial.name: Excellence Fund for Researchers
private_and_partial.benefits: Research grants covering laboratory fees and field-work expenses.
private_and_partial.target: Doctoral students and researchers in STEM or Albanian heritage studies.

english_language_test_requirements_2026.overview: Albanian universities offering English-taught programs require proof of proficiency. For the Type D visa, the Ministry of Interior validates these for student status.

duolingo_det.acceptance_note: Widely accepted by private international universities in Albania for initial admission.

toefl_ibt.note: Required by most English-medium institutions; must be verifiable online.

financial_solvency_thresholds.self_funded_min_balance: BDT 1,200,000 - 1,800,000 (Covering tuition + living costs of approx. €300/month + travel).
financial_solvency_thresholds.sponsor_profile: Parents or legal guardians. Must demonstrate a monthly income or savings sufficient to cover the student's stay in Albania.
financial_solvency_thresholds.bank_history_requirement: 3 to 6 months of active bank history. Notarized affidavit of support is mandatory for the visa application.
financial_solvency_thresholds.logic: The 'Sufficient Means of Subsistence' rule ensures students can sustain themselves without illegal employment during their studies.

embassy_submission_docs: Valid Passport (Valid for at least 3 months beyond visa expiry)
Certificate of Admission (Letter of Acceptance) from the University
Proof of Accommodation in Albania (Lease agreement or Dorm certificate)
Digital Photo (47mm x 36mm, White background)
Criminal Record Clearance (Police Clearance) from home country
Travel Health Insurance (Valid for the duration of stay)
coe_phase_documents: Proof of Tuition Fee Payment (Bank Transfer Receipt)
Notarized Financial Guarantee (Affidavit of Support)
Academic Diploma/Transcripts (Apostilled or legalized by Ministry)
English or Albanian Language Proficiency Certificate
Medical Certificate showing absence of infectious diseases
Visa Application Form (Completed via E-Albania portal)

processing_and_approval_metrics.visa_processing_time: 15 - 30 days (Processing via the E-Albania portal and Consulate)
processing_and_approval_metrics.approval_chances: High for students with authenticated documents and clear admission letters.
processing_and_approval_metrics.interview_probability: Medium. Usually conducted at the nearest Albanian Embassy/Consulate to verify intent.

high_risk_reasons: Submission of forged or non-apostilled academic documents.
Inability to prove accommodation arrangements in Albania.
Police clearance certificate that is older than 6 months.
Insufficient funds in the sponsor's account compared to the cost of living.
Failure to register on the E-Albania portal correctly.
rejection_risk_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Ensure all documents are translated into Albanian by a licensed translator and legalized by the relevant Ministry of Foreign Affairs.

work_rights_and_stay.part_time_permission: Up to 20 hours per week during the semester for international students.
work_rights_and_stay.vacation_hours: Full-time work allowed during summer and winter breaks.
work_rights_and_stay.visa_extension: Must apply for a Residence Permit (Leje Qëndrimi) within 30 days of arrival in Albania.
work_rights_and_stay.post_study_work: Eligible to apply for a 9-month job-seeking permit upon graduation from an Albanian University.

step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_1: Apply to an accredited Albanian University and receive a formal Letter of Acceptance.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_2: Pay the required tuition deposit and obtain a receipt for the visa file.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_3: Legalize/Apostille academic certificates and police clearance in your home country.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_4: Apply for the Long-Stay Type D/ST Visa via the E-Albania online portal.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_5: Submit physical documents to the Albanian Embassy/Consulate for biometric verification.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_6: Travel to Albania and register with the local police/migration office within 30 days.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_7: Apply for the physical Residence Permit card (valid for 1 year, renewable).

faq_student_edition.question: Can I study in Albania in English?
faq_student_edition.answer: Yes, many private and some public universities offer programs in English, particularly in Business, IT, and International Relations.

faq_student_edition.question: Do I need to translate my documents?
faq_student_edition.answer: Yes, all documents for the visa and residence permit must be translated into Albanian and notarized.

faq_student_edition.question: Is the Albanian student visa a Schengen visa?
faq_student_edition.answer: No, Albania is not in the Schengen Area yet. A Type D visa allows stay in Albania only, though you may visit nearby Balkan countries based on your nationality.

united_kingdom.title: United Kingdom Student Visa & Graduate Route Guide 2026 for International Students
united_kingdom.description: A complete roadmap for international students seeking higher education in the UK. Covering the CAS and Student Visa (formerly Tier 4) process, GREAT and Commonwealth scholarships, English requirements (IELTS UKVI, PTE Academic), financial capacity, and 2026 approval metrics.

visa_category_details.entry_type: Multiple Entry (Valid for the duration of the course plus a wrap-up period)
visa_category_details.eligibility: Applicants with a valid Confirmation of Acceptance for Studies (CAS) from a licensed Student Sponsor and who achieve 70 points under the Points-Based System.

government_funded.name: Chevening Scholarships
government_funded.benefits: Full tuition, monthly stipend, travel costs, and arrival/homeward departure allowances.
government_funded.application_window: August–November annually (for the following year's intake).
government_funded.selection_criteria: Leadership potential, strong academic background, and commitment to returning home to lead change.

private_and_partial.name: GREAT Scholarships
private_and_partial.benefits: Minimum of £10,000 towards tuition fees for a one-year postgraduate course.
private_and_partial.target: Students from specific countries (including Bangladesh) studying at participating UK universities.

private_and_partial.name: Commonwealth Master’s Scholarships
private_and_partial.benefits: Full tuition fees, airfare, and a monthly living allowance.
private_and_partial.special_note: Targeted at talented individuals from low and middle-income Commonwealth countries.

private_and_partial.name: University-Specific Global Excellence Awards
private_and_partial.benefits: Range from £2,000 to full tuition fee waivers.
private_and_partial.target: High-achieving students applying to universities like Exeter, Manchester, or UCL.

english_language_test_requirements_2026.overview: The UK Home Office requires proof of English proficiency. While universities may assess their own students for degree-level courses, specific SEVTs are often required.

duolingo_det.acceptance_note: Accepted by many universities for admission, but check if the university uses it to vouch for your level on the CAS.

toefl_ibt.note: Accepted by most UK universities; must be the Home Edition or standard iBT.

financial_solvency_thresholds.self_funded_min_balance: BDT 4,500,000 - 6,500,000 (Equivalent to remaining 1st year tuition + living costs of £1,334/mo in London or £1,023/mo outside London).
financial_solvency_thresholds.sponsor_profile: Self, Parents, or legal guardians. Third-party sponsors are generally not accepted unless they are official financial sponsors (government/international orgs).
financial_solvency_thresholds.bank_history_requirement: Funds must be held in the account for a consecutive 28-day period ending no more than 31 days before the application date.
financial_solvency_thresholds.logic: The '28-Day Rule' is strictly enforced to ensure the money is genuinely available and not borrowed temporarily for the visa application.

embassy_submission_docs: Valid Passport
Confirmation of Acceptance for Studies (CAS) Statement
Tuberculosis (TB) Test Certificate from an approved clinic
Digital Photo (Standard UK Passport size)
Proof of Financial Support (Bank statements held for 28 days)
ATAS Certificate (If required for specific STEM/Research courses)
coe_phase_documents: Academic Certificates and Transcripts listed on the CAS
English Language Proficiency Certificate (IELTS/PTE)
Immigration Health Surcharge (IHS) Payment Confirmation
Consent Letter from parents (if student is under 18)
Previous BRP or Visa copies (if applying from within or previously in the UK)

processing_and_approval_metrics.coe_processing_time: 1 - 4 weeks (University credibility interview and CAS issuance)
processing_and_approval_metrics.visa_processing_time: 15 working days (Standard) or 5 working days (Priority service available in most regions)
processing_and_approval_metrics.approval_chances: High if the CAS is valid and the 28-day financial rule is met perfectly.
processing_and_approval_metrics.interview_probability: Medium. Credibility interviews by UKVI are used to verify the student's genuine intent to study.

high_risk_reasons: Failing the 28-day financial rule by even a single day or a small currency fluctuation.
Inability to explain the course choice or career path during the credibility interview.
Submitting documents that are not in English without a professional translation.
Significant and unexplained gaps in education history.
Failure to disclose previous visa refusals from any country.
rejection_risk_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Double-check bank statements against the OANDA exchange rate on the day of application and prepare a thorough Statement of Purpose.

work_rights_and_stay.part_time_permission: Up to 20 hours per week during term time for degree-level students.
work_rights_and_stay.vacation_hours: Full-time work allowed during official university vacation periods.
work_rights_and_stay.visa_extension: Possible to extend onshore if moving to a new course that shows academic progression.
work_rights_and_stay.post_study_work: Eligible for the Graduate Route (PSW) visa, allowing 2 years (3 years for PhD) of work in the UK after graduation.

step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_1: Apply to a UK university and receive a Conditional/Unconditional Offer.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_2: Meet all offer conditions (English, Financials) and pass the university's Pre-CAS interview.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_3: Pay the tuition deposit to the university to trigger the issuance of the CAS.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_4: Book and complete the TB Test at a UKVI-approved medical center.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_5: Ensure funds have matured for 28 days, then complete the online visa application.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_6: Pay the Visa Fee (approx. £490) and the Immigration Health Surcharge (IHS).
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_7: Attend the VFS Global appointment for biometrics and wait for the passport return.

faq_student_edition.question: What is the Immigration Health Surcharge (IHS)?
faq_student_edition.answer: It is a mandatory fee paid during the visa application that allows international students to use the National Health Service (NHS) in the UK.

faq_student_edition.question: Can I bring my family (dependants)?
faq_student_edition.answer: As of January 2024, only students on postgraduate research programs (like PhDs) or government-sponsored courses can bring dependants.

faq_student_edition.question: Do I need to pay the full tuition before the visa?
faq_student_edition.answer: No, you only need to pay the deposit required by the university. However, the remaining balance must be shown in your bank account for 28 days.

germany.title: Germany Student Visa & Residency Guide 2026 for International Students
germany.description: A complete roadmap for international students seeking higher education in Germany. Covering the APS process, Blocked Account (Sperrkonto) requirements, DAAD scholarships, English/German language proficiency, and 2026 work-hour updates.

visa_category_details.entry_type: Multiple Entry (Converted to a Residence Permit within 90 days)
visa_category_details.eligibility: Applicants with a valid University Admission Letter (Zulassungsbescheid) and an APS Certificate (where applicable, e.g., Bangladesh, India, China).

government_funded.name: DAAD Scholarships (EPOS & Study Scholarships)
government_funded.benefits: Monthly stipend of €934 - €1,200, health insurance, travel allowance, and a one-time study grant.
government_funded.application_window: Varies by program; typically October–December for the following winter semester.
government_funded.selection_criteria: Strong academic record, professional experience (for EPOS), and a convincing motivation letter.

private_and_partial.name: Deutschlandstipendium
private_and_partial.benefits: €300 per month (co-funded by the government and private sponsors).
private_and_partial.target: High-achieving students regardless of nationality or income level.

private_and_partial.name: Erasmus+ Key Action 1
private_and_partial.benefits: Monthly living allowance (approx. €450–€600) for exchange semesters.
private_and_partial.special_note: Available to students at partner universities outside the EU for short-term study.

private_and_partial.name: Friedrich Ebert / Konrad Adenauer Foundations
private_and_partial.benefits: Monthly stipend and social/political networking opportunities.
private_and_partial.target: Students with excellent grades and a commitment to social/political values.

english_language_test_requirements_2026.overview: German universities strictly follow the CEFR standards. English-taught programs require IELTS/PTE, while German-taught programs require TestDaF or DSH.

duolingo_det.acceptance_note: Accepted by several private universities and some public applied science universities.

toefl_ibt.note: Widely accepted; scores must be verifiable via the ETS portal.

financial_solvency_thresholds.self_funded_min_balance: BDT 1,500,000 - 1,600,000 (Equivalent to the mandatory Blocked Account amount of €11,904).
financial_solvency_thresholds.sponsor_profile: Immediate family members via a 'Verpflichtungserklärung' (Formal obligation letter) signed at a German Aliens' Authority or Embassy.
financial_solvency_thresholds.bank_history_requirement: For Blocked Accounts (Fintiba, Expatrio, Coracle), funds must be deposited before the visa interview. No 3-month history is required if using a Blocked Account.
financial_solvency_thresholds.logic: The 'Sperrkonto' (Blocked Account) ensures that the student has exactly €992 per month available for living expenses throughout the first year.

embassy_submission_docs: Valid Passport
Admission Letter (Zulassungsbescheid)
APS Certificate (Original)
Blocked Account Confirmation (Blocking Confirmation)
Motivation Letter (LOM) - Personally written and signed
Biometric Photos (35mm x 45mm, Neutral background)
coe_phase_documents: Academic Degrees & Transcripts (Notarized & Translated if needed)
Curriculum Vitae (CV) in tabular format
Proof of Language Proficiency (IELTS/TestDaF)
Travel Health Insurance (Valid until enrollment)
Proof of Application Fee payment (if via Uni-Assist)

processing_and_approval_metrics.visa_processing_time: 4 - 6 weeks (Coordination between the Embassy and the local Ausländerbehörde in Germany)
processing_and_approval_metrics.approval_chances: Very high if academic credentials are verified via APS and the Blocked Account is funded.
processing_and_approval_metrics.interview_probability: High. Embassy officers conduct interviews to verify the student's motivation and study plan.

high_risk_reasons: Inability to explain the choice of a specific city or university in Germany.
Significant academic gaps without a valid explanation or professional certificate.
Applying for a Master's degree that is not 'consecutive' (unrelated to Bachelor's).
Inadequate German language skills for programs with a German-taught component.
Submitting a generic Motivation Letter copied from the internet.
rejection_risk_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Focus the Motivation Letter on the German university's specific research focus and how the degree will help your career back in your home country.

work_rights_and_stay.part_time_permission: 140 full days or 280 half days per year (Increased in 2024/2026).
work_rights_and_stay.vacation_hours: Full-time work allowed during semester breaks (Vorlesungsfreie Zeit).
work_rights_and_stay.visa_extension: Students must apply for a Residence Permit (Aufenthaltstitel) at the local Foreigners' Office after arriving.
work_rights_and_stay.post_study_work: Eligible for an 18-month Job-Seeker Visa after graduation to find a role related to your studies.

step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_1: Apply for APS verification (mandatory for Bangladesh/India) to certify your academic documents.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_2: Submit university applications via Uni-Assist or directly to the University portal.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_3: Open a Blocked Account (e.g., Expatrio, Fintiba) and transfer €11,904.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_4: Book a visa appointment at the German Embassy (slots can be limited; book early).
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_5: Attend the interview with all original documents and the APS certificate.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_6: Wait for visa approval and travel to Germany (usually allowed to enter 2 weeks before semester).
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_7: Register your address (Anmeldung) and convert your visa to a Residence Permit.

faq_student_edition.question: Are there tuition fees at public universities?
faq_student_edition.answer: Most public universities have zero tuition fees, but you must pay a 'Semester Contribution' (approx. €200–€400) which includes a public transport ticket.

faq_student_edition.question: Do I need to speak German to get a visa?
faq_student_edition.answer: For English-taught programs, no. However, basic German (A1/A2) is highly recommended for daily life and is viewed positively by visa officers.

faq_student_edition.question: Can I use the Blocked Account money immediately?
faq_student_edition.answer: No, you can only withdraw €992 per month after you arrive in Germany and open a local bank account to link with the blocked one.

malaysia.title: Malaysia Student Visa & EMGS Guide 2026 for International Students
malaysia.description: A complete roadmap for international students seeking higher education in Malaysia. Covering the EMGS VAL process, eVAL/eVisa stages, MIS and university scholarships, English requirements (IELTS, PTE, Linguaskill), financial capacity, and 2026 approval metrics.

visa_category_details.visa_type: Student Pass (Social Visit Pass for entry)
visa_category_details.entry_type: Single Entry Visa (SEV) to enter, Multiple Entry Student Pass after endorsement
visa_category_details.eligibility: Applicants with a valid University Offer Letter and a Visa Approval Letter (VAL) issued by Education Malaysia Global Services (EMGS).

government_funded.name: Malaysia International Scholarship (MIS)
government_funded.benefits: Full tuition fees and a monthly living allowance of approx. RM 1,500.
government_funded.selection_criteria: Outstanding academic records (GPA 3.0+), research proposal (for Master/PhD), and leadership potential.

private_and_partial.name: MTCP Scholarship
private_and_partial.benefits: Tuition fees, living allowance, book/tool allowance, and return air tickets.
private_and_partial.target: Postgraduate students from developing countries under the Malaysian Technical Cooperation Programme.

private_and_partial.name: Vice-Chancellor/President’s Merit Awards
private_and_partial.benefits: 25% to 100% tuition fee waivers.
private_and_partial.special_note: Offered by top private universities like Taylor’s, Sunway, and APU for high-achieving applicants.

private_and_partial.name: Graduate Research Assistantship (GRA)
private_and_partial.benefits: Monthly stipend (RM 2,000 - 3,000) and tuition fee waivers.
private_and_partial.target: Master by Research and PhD candidates at Public Universities (UM, UPM, USM).

english_language_test_requirements_2026.overview: EMGS and Malaysian universities require English proficiency for Student Pass issuance. Some students may enter for English courses (IEP) first if they don't meet the primary goal.

duolingo_det.acceptance_note: Accepted by most private universities for admission and EMGS processing.

toefl_ibt.note: Widely accepted; MyBest scores are generally not accepted for visa purposes.

financial_solvency_thresholds.self_funded_min_balance: BDT 1,000,000 - 1,500,000 (Equivalent to 1st year tuition + approx. USD 5,000 for living costs).
financial_solvency_thresholds.sponsor_profile: Self, Parents, or close relatives. Must provide a bank solvency certificate and 3-6 months of bank statements.
financial_solvency_thresholds.bank_history_requirement: 3 months of stable history. Some universities require the full first-year tuition to be paid before issuing the final VAL support.
financial_solvency_thresholds.logic: The 'Financial Capability' check ensures the student can afford the low cost of living in Malaysia without depending on illegal work.

embassy_submission_docs: Valid Passport (Minimum 18 months validity remaining)
eVAL (Electronic Visa Approval Letter) from EMGS
Offer Letter from a Malaysian Educational Institution
Digital Photo (35mm x 45mm, White background)
Yellow Fever Vaccination Certificate (if coming from high-risk zones)
eVisa / SEV (Single Entry Visa) approval printout
coe_phase_documents: All Passport Pages (including blank pages) scanned in color
Academic Certificates & Transcripts (Attested by Ministry of Education)
Health Declaration Form (Self-declaration for EMGS application)
English Test Report (IELTS/PTE/TOEFL)
Personal Bond payment receipt (collected by the university)
Pre-arrival Medical Screening Report

processing_and_approval_metrics.coe_processing_time: 2 - 4 weeks (University screening and EMGS application creation)
processing_and_approval_metrics.visa_processing_time: 14 - 21 working days (For eVAL issuance); 2-5 days for eVisa after eVAL.
processing_and_approval_metrics.approval_chances: Very high (95%+) if academic documents are genuine and health declaration is clear.
processing_and_approval_metrics.interview_probability: Very Low. Most applications are processed digitally based on documents.

high_risk_reasons: Passport validity less than 18 months at the time of application.
Poor quality scans of passport pages or incorrect photo dimensions (35x45mm).
Discrepancies between names on academic certificates and the passport.
Medical conditions listed as 'unfit' (e.g., Hepatitis B/C, HIV, Tuberculosis).
Unexplained study gaps exceeding 3-5 years for undergraduate applicants.
rejection_risk_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Ensure all documents are high-resolution color scans. Use the EMGS mobile app to track 'Yellow' and 'Green' status percentages daily.

work_rights_and_stay.part_time_permission: Up to 20 hours per week, but ONLY during semester breaks or holidays of more than 7 days.
work_rights_and_stay.vacation_hours: Full-time work is still technically restricted; focus is on the 'Student' status.
work_rights_and_stay.visa_extension: Renewable annually onshore. The university's visa office handles the Student Pass renewal.
work_rights_and_stay.post_study_work: Eligible for the 'Social Visit Pass (Long Term)' for 1 year if graduated from selected top universities (Graduate Pass).

step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_1: Apply to a Malaysian University and receive your 'Letter of Offer'.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_2: University applies for your VAL (Visa Approval Letter) through the EMGS portal.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_3: Complete the 'Health Declaration' and pay the EMGS processing fees (approx. RM 2,000 - 3,000).
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_4: Once eVAL is issued (70%-80% on EMGS tracking), apply for an eVisa (Single Entry Visa) online.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_5: Travel to Malaysia and clear immigration using your eVAL and eVisa.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_6: Within 7 days of arrival, undergo the 'Post-Arrival Medical Screening' at an EMGS panel clinic.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_7: Submit your passport to the university to get the 'Student Pass' sticker (Endorsement).

faq_student_edition.question: Can I work while studying in Malaysia in 2026?
faq_student_edition.answer: Work is strictly limited to 20 hours per week during semester breaks only. International students are not allowed to work during the active semester.

faq_student_edition.question: What is the 'EMGS Tracking' percentage?
faq_student_edition.answer: It is a digital progress bar. 35% means EMGS approval, and 70% means the Immigration Department has issued your eVAL.

faq_student_edition.question: Is the medical check-up mandatory?
faq_student_edition.answer: Yes. You must pass both a pre-arrival declaration and a strict post-arrival medical test (blood/urine/X-ray) in Malaysia.

netherlands.title: Netherlands Student Visa & Orientation Year Guide 2026 for International Students
netherlands.description: A complete roadmap for international students seeking higher education in the Netherlands. Covering the MVV and VVR process via the TEV procedure, Holland and Orange Knowledge scholarships, English requirements (IELTS, PTE), financial capacity, and 2026 post-study orientation metrics.

visa_category_details.entry_type: Single Entry (MVV) to enter, converted to Multiple Entry Residence Permit upon arrival
visa_category_details.eligibility: Applicants must be admitted to a recognized educational institution (sponsor) and meet the study progress requirements (50% of ECTS credits annually).

government_funded.name: NL Scholarship (formerly Holland Scholarship)
government_funded.benefits: One-time grant of €5,000, €10,000, or €15,000 for the first year of study.
government_funded.selection_criteria: Non-EEA nationality, excellence in academics, and application to a participating research or applied sciences university.

private_and_partial.name: Orange Tulip Scholarship (OTS)
private_and_partial.benefits: Varies by institution; typically partial to full tuition fee waivers.
private_and_partial.target: Talented students from specific countries including Vietnam, India, and Indonesia.

private_and_partial.name: Erasmus+ Study Grant
private_and_partial.benefits: Monthly stipend (approx. €450 - €600) for exchange students from partner universities.
private_and_partial.special_note: Aims to promote mobility between European and non-European higher education institutions.

private_and_partial.name: University Excellence Scholarships
private_and_partial.benefits: Full tuition waivers plus a modest living stipend.
private_and_partial.target: Offered by universities like TU Delft (Justus & Louise van Effen) and Utrecht (Utrecht Excellence Scholarship).

english_language_test_requirements_2026.overview: Dutch universities require a high level of English for international programs. The IND accepts standard tests, but universities set specific minimum thresholds for admission.

duolingo_det.acceptance_note: Accepted by many Universities of Applied Sciences (HBO) but less common for Research Universities (WO).

toefl_ibt.note: Most institutions do not accept the Home Edition; scores must be from a test center.

financial_solvency_thresholds.self_funded_min_balance: BDT 1,600,000 - 2,000,000 (Equivalent to the IND requirement of approx. €1,218/month for living costs + 1st year tuition).
financial_solvency_thresholds.sponsor_profile: Self-funded or a legal resident in the Netherlands/abroad. Notarized financial guarantee is required if someone else is paying.
financial_solvency_thresholds.bank_history_requirement: Funds must be 'freely accessible'. Universities often require students to transfer the full living amount and tuition to the university's account as a deposit.
financial_solvency_thresholds.logic: The 'Proof of Financial Means' ensures students can focus on their studies without needing to work more than the allowed 16 hours per week.

embassy_submission_docs: Valid Passport
MVV Issue Form (MVV-aanvraagformulier)
Digital Photo (compliant with Dutch passport standards)
Admission Letter from a Dutch Higher Education Institution
Tuberculosis (TB) test commitment (for specific countries)
Biometric data collection receipt
coe_phase_documents: Academic Diplomas & Transcripts (Translated into English/Dutch/German/French)
Proof of Financial Means (Bank statements or Scholarship award)
Antecedents Certificate (Background check/Criminal record declaration)
Proof of English Proficiency (IELTS/PTE)
Proof of Health Insurance (Private or Aon Student Insurance)
Legalized Birth Certificate (often required for local municipality registration)

processing_and_approval_metrics.coe_processing_time: 2 - 6 weeks (University assessment and institutional sponsorship approval)
processing_and_approval_metrics.visa_processing_time: 60 - 90 days (Total TEV procedure time via the IND)
processing_and_approval_metrics.approval_chances: Very high if the sponsoring university approves the application and financial deposit is paid.
processing_and_approval_metrics.interview_probability: Very Low. The process is primarily document-based and managed by the university's international office.

high_risk_reasons: Failure to meet the 'Study Progress' rule (less than 50% credits) if already in the NL.
Providing bank statements where funds are not 'freely accessible' or are in a frozen account.
Incomplete legalization of documents (Apostille or Embassy legalization missing).
Discrepancies between the application details and the Antecedents Certificate.
Missing the strict university deadline for document submission (often June/July).
rejection_risk_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Use the university's pre-screening service and ensure the 'Proof of Financial Means' is submitted as a direct transfer to the university trust account if offered.

work_rights_and_stay.part_time_permission: Up to 16 hours per week throughout the year.
work_rights_and_stay.vacation_hours: Full-time work allowed only in June, July, and August.
work_rights_and_stay.visa_extension: The VVR is valid for the duration of the course + 3 months; handled by the university.
work_rights_and_stay.post_study_work: Eligible for the 'Orientation Year' (Zoekjaar) permit, giving 1 year to find a job as a highly skilled migrant.

step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_1: Apply to a Dutch university and receive an Unconditional Admission Letter.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_2: The University initiates the TEV procedure (MVV and VVR) on your behalf.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_3: Pay the tuition fees and living expenses deposit directly to the university's bank account.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_4: Upload all required legal documents (Birth Cert, Passport, Financial proof) to the university portal.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_5: Wait for the IND approval letter; then book an appointment at the Dutch Embassy to collect the MVV sticker.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_6: Travel to the Netherlands and register at the local City Hall (Gemeente) to get your BSN.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_7: Collect your biometric Residence Permit (VVR) card from the IND office.

faq_student_edition.question: What is the BSN and why do I need it?
faq_student_edition.answer: The BSN (Burgerservicenummer) is your unique citizen service number. You need it to open a bank account, work, and use healthcare in the Netherlands.

faq_student_edition.question: Can I switch to a work visa after my studies?
faq_student_edition.answer: Yes. Upon graduation, you can apply for a one-year 'Orientation Year' permit. If you find a job with a recognized sponsor, you can switch to a Highly Skilled Migrant visa.

faq_student_edition.question: Is insurance mandatory for the visa?
faq_student_edition.answer: Yes. You must have adequate health and liability insurance. Most students use specialized providers like Aon or IPS until they start working.

france.title: France Student Visa & Campus France Guide 2026 for International Students
france.description: A complete roadmap for international students seeking higher education in France. Covering the Études en France (EEF) procedure, VLS-TS visa validation, Eiffel Excellence scholarships, language requirements (DELF/IELTS), financial capacity, and 2026 residency updates.

visa_category_details.entry_type: Multiple Entry (Valid as a residence permit once validated online)
visa_category_details.eligibility: Applicants with an official acceptance letter from a French institution and completion of the Campus France 'Études en France' procedure (for applicable countries).

government_funded.name: France Excellence Eiffel Scholarship
government_funded.benefits: Monthly allowance (€1,181 for Master / €1,800 for PhD), return airfare, social security, and cultural activities.
government_funded.application_window: October–January annually (application submitted by the French institution).
government_funded.selection_criteria: Academic excellence (top 10%), international experience, and priority fields like STEM, Law, or Economics.

private_and_partial.name: Erasmus+ Mobility Grants
private_and_partial.benefits: Monthly stipend ranging from €300 to €600 for exchange students within Europe.
private_and_partial.target: Students enrolled in European partner universities participating in credit mobility.

private_and_partial.name: Charpak Scholarship (Regional Focus)
private_and_partial.benefits: Tuition fee waiver up to €5,000 and living allowance for students from specific regions like India.
private_and_partial.special_note: Includes a simplified visa procedure and priority for university housing (CROUS).

private_and_partial.name: Institutional Merit Scholarships
private_and_partial.benefits: Varies; often provides 20% to 50% tuition reduction for the first year.
private_and_partial.target: Offered by Business Schools (HEC, INSEAD) and private engineering schools based on GMAT/GRE scores.

english_language_test_requirements_2026.overview: For English-taught programs, French proficiency is not mandatory for the visa, but English scores are required. For French-taught programs, DELF/DALF is essential.

duolingo_det.acceptance_note: Accepted by an increasing number of Business and Design schools; check university-specific lists.

toefl_ibt.note: Widely accepted; some institutions may require specific section minimums (e.g., 20 in Writing).

financial_solvency_thresholds.self_funded_min_balance: BDT 1,200,000 - 1,500,000 (Equivalent to €615/month for the full academic year + tuition fees).
financial_solvency_thresholds.sponsor_profile: Parents or legal guardians. Must provide a signed financial guarantee (Attestation de prise en charge) and proof of professional income.
financial_solvency_thresholds.bank_history_requirement: 3 to 6 months of bank statements showing regular income. Blocked accounts (Studely/Fintiba) are highly recommended for faster processing.
financial_solvency_thresholds.logic: The 'Minimum Resource' rule ensures students can cover basic living costs without relying on part-time work, which is capped by law.

embassy_submission_docs: Valid Passport (Valid for at least 3 months after visa expiry)
France-Visas Application Receipt & Form
EEF (Études en France) Confirmation / Campus France ID
Official Acceptance Letter (Attestation d'admission)
Proof of Accommodation for the first 3 months (Lease/CROUS letter/Host letter)
Digital Photo (35mm x 45mm, ISO/IEC standards)
coe_phase_documents: Academic Diplomas & Transcripts (Certified translations if not in English/French)
CV and Motivation Letter (Study plan)
Language Proficiency Certificate (IELTS/PTE or DELF/TCF)
Birth Certificate (Legalized/Apostilled for future CAF applications)
Travel Insurance (Schengen-compliant for the first 90 days)
Financial Guarantee Documents (Statements/Sponsor IDs)

processing_and_approval_metrics.coe_processing_time: 2 - 5 weeks (Campus France academic interview and document validation)
processing_and_approval_metrics.approval_chances: High for students with a consistent academic path and clear professional goals.
processing_and_approval_metrics.interview_probability: High. The Campus France interview is mandatory for most students to evaluate academic intent.

high_risk_reasons: Lack of coherence between the chosen course and previous academic background.
Inadequate accommodation proof (e.g., hotel booking for only 2 days).
Inability to justify study gaps or lack of career progression.
Sponsor's income insufficient to cover the €615/month requirement after their own expenses.
Poor performance during the Campus France interview (Language or Motivation).
rejection_risk_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Highlight the ROI of a French degree in your home country and secure a 'Certificat d'hébergement' if staying with a host.

work_rights_and_stay.part_time_permission: Up to 964 hours per year (approx. 60% of full-time legal work).
work_rights_and_stay.vacation_hours: Full-time work allowed during university holidays.
work_rights_and_stay.visa_extension: Must validate the VLS-TS online within 3 months of arrival. Renewal is done at the local Prefecture.
work_rights_and_stay.post_study_work: Eligible for a 1-year Temporary Residence Permit (RECE) to find a job or start a business after graduation.

step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_1: Create an account on the 'Études en France' (EEF) platform and upload your academic dossier.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_2: Attend the mandatory Campus France academic interview (online or in-person).
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_3: Receive the 'Accord de la commission' (Campus France approval) once the interview is validated.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_4: Complete the online application on the France-Visas portal and pay the visa fee (€50 - €99).
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_5: Book an appointment at VFS Global or TLScontact for biometric submission.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_6: Collect your visa and, within 3 months of arrival in France, validate your VLS-TS on the ANEF portal.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_7: Register for the French Social Security (L'Assurance Maladie) and apply for CAF housing benefits.

faq_student_edition.question: What is CAF and can international students apply?
faq_student_edition.answer: CAF is the French housing subsidy. Most international students are eligible to receive a monthly refund (approx. €100–€200) on their rent.

faq_student_edition.question: Do I need to speak French to get a student visa?
faq_student_edition.answer: No, if your program is taught in English. However, having an A1/A2 level certificate is viewed very positively during the interview.

faq_student_edition.question: Is the student visa also a residency permit?
faq_student_edition.answer: Yes. The VLS-TS serves as a residence permit, but only after you validate it online and pay the fiscal stamp fee (€50) after arriving.
