russia.title: Russia Student Visa & University Enrollment Guide 2026 for International Students
russia.description: A complete roadmap for international students seeking higher education in Russia. Covering the GUVM/MIA Invitation process, Russian Government Scholarships (Education in Russia), HIV/AIDS certification requirements, financial capacity, and 2026 migration registration rules.

visa_category_details.entry_type: Single Entry (Initially 90 days, extended to Multiple Entry upon arrival)
visa_category_details.eligibility: Applicants with an official invitation letter issued by the Ministry of Internal Affairs (MIA) or the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MFA) via the host university.

government_funded.name: Russian Government Scholarship (Quota)
government_funded.benefits: Full tuition coverage, monthly maintenance allowance (stipend), and reduced-cost dormitory housing.
government_funded.application_window: September–January annually (via the 'Education in Russia' portal).
government_funded.selection_criteria: Academic merit, competitive entrance exams, and successful interview at the local Russian House (Rossotrudnichestvo).

private_and_partial.name: Open Doors: Russian Scholarship Project
private_and_partial.benefits: Tuition-free enrollment in Master's or PhD programs at top Russian universities (Global Universities Association).
private_and_partial.target: Talented international students competing in subject-specific Olympiads.
private_and_partial.special_note: Winners can apply to any university in Russia without entrance exams.

private_and_partial.name: University-Specific Merit Grants
private_and_partial.benefits: Varies from 25% to 75% tuition discount for the first year.
private_and_partial.target: High-achieving students at institutions like HSE University, ITMO, or RUDN.

private_and_partial.name: Free Preparatory Year
private_and_partial.benefits: Zero-cost intensive Russian language and subject-specific training before the degree starts.
private_and_partial.target: All government quota recipients who do not speak Russian at a B2 level.

english_language_test_requirements_2026.overview: For programs taught in English, most universities require proof of proficiency. However, if studying in Russian after a preparatory year, no external English test is required.

duolingo_det.acceptance_note: Accepted by several international-facing universities like HSE or Innopolis for admission.

toefl_ibt.note: Standard iBT is widely accepted; certificates must be provided during the university admission phase.

financial_solvency_thresholds.self_funded_min_balance: BDT 800,000 - 1,200,000 (Equivalent to 1st year tuition + approx. $3,000 - $5,000 for living costs).
financial_solvency_thresholds.sponsor_profile: Self, Parents, or legal guardians. Must provide a bank statement and a sponsorship letter (translated into Russian if required).
financial_solvency_thresholds.bank_history_requirement: Current balance must reflect the ability to pay for the first year. Russia is generally flexible on the 'history' length but strict on the total amount.
financial_solvency_thresholds.logic: The 'Financial Guarantee' check ensures students can afford the affordable cost of living in Russia without illegal employment.

embassy_submission_docs: Valid Passport (Minimum 18 months validity from visa start date)
Original Invitation Letter (Paper or Electronic MIA format)
HIV/AIDS Test Certificate (Negative, issued within the last 3 months)
Visa Application Form (Completed at visa.kdmid.ru)
One Photo (35mm x 45mm, White background)
Academic Certificates (Legalized/Apostilled and translated into Russian)
coe_phase_documents: Standard Medical Health Certificate (Proof of fitness to study)
Chest X-ray (Tuberculosis clearance report)
Proof of Relationship with Sponsor (Birth certificate/ID)
English or Russian Language Proficiency proof
Tuition Fee Payment Receipt (for self-funded students)
Travel Health Insurance (Valid in the Russian Federation)

processing_and_approval_metrics.coe_processing_time: 30 - 45 days (MIA Invitation processing time after university approval)
processing_and_approval_metrics.visa_processing_time: 4 - 20 working days (Consulate processing time depending on the urgency fee paid)
processing_and_approval_metrics.approval_chances: Very high (98%+) provided the official MIA Invitation is genuine and the HIV test is negative.
processing_and_approval_metrics.interview_probability: Low. Interviews are rare unless there is a discrepancy in the motivation or documents.

high_risk_reasons: Passport validity less than 18 months at the time of the visa application.
Positive HIV test result (Mandatory ground for visa denial/deportation).
Expired HIV certificate (Must be within 3 months of the embassy date).
Discrepancies in the spelling of names between the Invitation and the Passport.
Failure to legalize/apostille academic documents in the home country.
rejection_risk_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Always use a specialized translation agency for the 'Notarized Russian Translation' of your degree and passport.

work_rights_and_stay.part_time_permission: Full-time students can work without a separate work permit during their spare time.
work_rights_and_stay.vacation_hours: Unlimited work permitted during official university holidays.
work_rights_and_stay.visa_extension: The initial 90-day visa must be extended to a 1-year multi-entry visa at the local migration office (GUVM) after arrival.
work_rights_and_stay.post_study_work: Eligible for a Simplified Permanent Residence Permit (VNR) or temporary stay for job-seeking after graduation from a state-accredited university.

step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_1: Apply to a Russian university and secure an 'Admission Letter'.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_2: Wait for the university to apply for your official Invitation via the Ministry of Internal Affairs (MIA).
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_3: Undergo the mandatory medical tests, specifically the HIV/AIDS test, at an approved clinic.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_4: Once the original/electronic Invitation arrives, complete the online form at visa.kdmid.ru.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_5: Submit your passport, invitation, and HIV certificate to the Russian Embassy/Consulate or Visa Center.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_6: Travel to Russia and complete 'Migration Registration' with your university within 7 business days.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_7: Submit documents to the university’s international office for the 1-year visa extension (Endorsement).

faq_student_edition.question: Is the HIV test really mandatory for the Russian visa?
faq_student_edition.answer: Yes. A negative HIV/AIDS test result is a federal requirement for all international student visas and must be presented at the embassy and for annual extensions.

faq_student_edition.question: Do I need to speak Russian to apply?
faq_student_edition.answer: No. You can apply for English-taught programs or enroll in a 'Podfak' (Preparatory Faculty) to learn Russian from scratch for one year.

faq_student_edition.question: What is Migration Registration?
faq_student_edition.answer: It is a legal notice that tells the government where you are living. Your university or dormitory must register you every time you enter the country or change cities.

turkey.title: Turkey Student Visa & Residence Permit Guide 2026 for International Students
turkey.description: A complete roadmap for international students seeking higher education in Turkey. Covering the Student Entry Visa, e-Ikamet (Residence Permit) process, Türkiye Bursları (Government Scholarships), English/Turkish proficiency requirements, financial capacity, and 2026 work-hour updates.

visa_category_details.visa_type: Student Entry Visa (Öğrenci Vizesi) & Residence Permit
visa_category_details.entry_type: Single Entry (Sticker visa for entry, converted to a Residence Permit card after arrival)
visa_category_details.eligibility: Applicants with an official Acceptance Letter from a Turkish university recognized by the Council of Higher Education (YÖK).

government_funded.name: Türkiye Scholarships (Türkiye Bursları)
government_funded.benefits: Full tuition, monthly stipend (₺4,500 - ₺9,000), free accommodation, health insurance, and return flight tickets.
government_funded.selection_criteria: Minimum academic GPA (70% for undergrad, 75% for grad, 90% for medicine) and successful interview performance.

private_and_partial.name: University Merit Discounts
private_and_partial.benefits: Range from 25% to 100% tuition fee reduction.
private_and_partial.target: High-achieving international students at private institutions like Sabancı, Bilkent, or Koç University.

private_and_partial.name: Islamic Development Bank (IsDB) - Türkiye Bursları Joint Program
private_and_partial.benefits: Full funding specifically for students from IsDB member countries.
private_and_partial.special_note: Focuses on sustainability-related fields and technology.

private_and_partial.name: TÜBİTAK Research Fellowships
private_and_partial.benefits: Monthly stipend and research grants.
private_and_partial.target: International PhD students and researchers in STEM fields.

english_language_test_requirements_2026.overview: For English-taught programs, universities require international test scores. If scores are missing, students must pass the university's internal Proficiency Exam or attend a one-year prep school.

duolingo_det.acceptance_note: Increasingly accepted by private foundation universities for direct entry into departments.

toefl_ibt.note: The most widely recognized test across both public and private Turkish universities.

financial_solvency_thresholds.self_funded_min_balance: BDT 800,000 - 1,200,000 (Equivalent to approx. $6,000 - $10,000 covering tuition + living costs of $500-600/month).
financial_solvency_thresholds.sponsor_profile: Parents or legal guardians. Must provide a notarized sponsorship letter and 3-6 months of bank statements.
financial_solvency_thresholds.bank_history_requirement: 3 months of active bank history. Statements must be stamped and issued within 15 days of the visa appointment.
financial_solvency_thresholds.logic: The 'Financial Guarantee' ensures students can sustain themselves given the rising cost of living and inflation in major cities like Istanbul and Ankara.

embassy_submission_docs: Valid Passport (at least 6 months validity remaining)
Official Acceptance Letter from the Turkish University
Biometric Photos (50mm x 50mm, White background)
Proof of Financial Means (Bank statements/Scholarship letter)
Travel Health Insurance (Valid for 90 days)
Completed Pre-Application Form from the Konsolosluk portal
coe_phase_documents: Academic Diplomas & Transcripts (Translated into Turkish and Notarized)
Equivalency Certificate (Denklik Belgesi) - can be initiated after arrival
Student Certificate (Öğrenci Belgesi) - obtained after university enrollment
Proof of Address in Turkey (Dormitory letter or notarized rental contract)
Police Clearance Certificate (if requested by the specific consulate)
Residence Permit Card Fee Receipt (approx. ₺964 - ₺3,500 including service)

processing_and_approval_metrics.visa_processing_time: 15 - 45 days (Depending on the volume at the Turkish Embassy/Consulate)
processing_and_approval_metrics.approval_chances: High for students with clear admission letters and valid financial documents.
processing_and_approval_metrics.interview_probability: Medium. Usually a brief interview at the Consulate regarding study plans and funding.

high_risk_reasons: Submitting an acceptance letter from an institution not recognized by YÖK.
Bank statements that do not show a consistent or sufficient balance for tuition.
Passport validity of less than 6 months at the time of entry.
Failure to apply for the Residence Permit (İkamet) within 30 days of arrival.
Using a tourist e-visa for a long-term academic program.
rejection_risk_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Ensure your sponsorship letter is notarized and translates clearly to Turkish or English. Always verify your university’s YÖK status before paying any deposit.

work_rights_and_stay.part_time_permission: International students can work up to 24 hours per week (post-2024 update).
work_rights_and_stay.vacation_hours: Full-time work is generally restricted; students are expected to maintain academic focus.
work_rights_and_stay.visa_extension: Must apply for a Student Residence Permit via the e-Ikamet portal within 30 days of arrival.
work_rights_and_stay.post_study_work: Eligible to apply for a 'Short-Term Residence Permit' for 1 year for job-seeking purposes after graduation.

step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_1: Apply to a Turkish university and receive your official 'Kabul Mektubu' (Acceptance Letter).
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_2: Complete the online pre-application on the Turkish Ministry of Foreign Affairs website.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_3: Attend your appointment at the Turkish Embassy/VFS Global with your financial and academic files.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_4: Once the entry visa is granted, travel to Turkey and complete your university enrollment.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_5: Apply for the 'Student Residence Permit' online via the e-Ikamet (goc.gov.tr) portal within 30 days.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_6: Attend the appointment at the Provincial Directorate of Migration Management (İl Göç İdaresi).
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_7: Receive your physical Residence Permit ID card via PTT (Turkish Post) at your registered address.

faq_student_edition.question: Do I need a student visa if I have a green passport or e-visa?
faq_student_edition.answer: Yes. Even if you can enter for tourism, you must enter with a 'Student Visa' to be eligible for a long-term Student Residence Permit.

faq_student_edition.question: Can I study in English in Turkey?
faq_student_edition.answer: Yes, many private and some major public universities (like METU or Boğaziçi) offer programs entirely in English.

faq_student_edition.question: Is health insurance mandatory for the residence permit?
faq_student_edition.answer: Yes. You must have either a private Turkish health insurance policy or register for the government SGK (General Health Insurance) after arrival.

hungary.title: Hungary Student Visa & Stipendium Hungaricum Guide 2026 for International Students
hungary.description: A complete roadmap for international students seeking higher education in Hungary. Covering the Residence Permit for Study Purposes (D-type visa), the prestigious Stipendium Hungaricum scholarship, English requirements (IELTS/TOEFL), financial solvency, and 2026 post-study work rights.

visa_category_details.visa_type: D-type Student Visa (Residence Permit for Study Purposes)
visa_category_details.entry_type: Single Entry (30 days to enter, converted to a Residence Permit card upon arrival)
visa_category_details.eligibility: Applicants with an Unconditional Letter of Acceptance from a recognized Hungarian Higher Education Institution (HEI) and proof of paid tuition or scholarship award.

government_funded.name: Stipendium Hungaricum Scholarship
government_funded.benefits: Full tuition waiver, monthly stipend (HUF 43,700 for BA/MA; up to HUF 180,000 for PhD), free dormitory or accommodation contribution (HUF 40,000/mo), and medical insurance.
government_funded.application_window: November – January 15 annually (via the Tempus Public Foundation portal).
government_funded.selection_criteria: Academic excellence, nomination by the home country's partner authority, and successful university entrance exams/interviews.

private_and_partial.name: Erasmus+ Key Action 1
private_and_partial.benefits: Monthly living grant (approx. €450 - €600) for exchange semesters at Hungarian partner universities.
private_and_partial.target: International students from partner countries outside the EU.

private_and_partial.name: Christian Young People Scholarship
private_and_partial.benefits: Full scholarship (similar to Stipendium Hungaricum) for students from specific regions experiencing humanitarian crises.
private_and_partial.target: Christian minority students for Bachelor, Master, and PhD levels.

private_and_partial.name: University Merit Discounts
private_and_partial.benefits: 10% to 50% tuition reduction for top-performing self-funded students.
private_and_partial.special_note: Common at private institutions like International Business School (IBS) or Corvinus University.

english_language_test_requirements_2026.overview: Hungarian universities are strict about CEFR B2 level proficiency for degree programs. For Stipendium Hungaricum, specific thresholds are set by the host university.

duolingo_det.acceptance_note: Widely accepted for initial admission and foundation years at most private and some public universities.

toefl_ibt.note: Acceptance varies by department; scores must be verifiable online.

financial_solvency_thresholds.self_funded_min_balance: BDT 1,200,000 - 1,500,000 (Equivalent to approx. €10,000 covering 1st year tuition + living costs of €600-800/month).
financial_solvency_thresholds.sponsor_profile: Self, Parents, or legal guardians. Must provide a notarized letter of guarantee and 6 months of bank statements.
financial_solvency_thresholds.bank_history_requirement: 6 months of stable transactions. Sudden large deposits without explanation may lead to refusal.
financial_solvency_thresholds.logic: The 'Sufficient Subsistence' rule requires students to show they can live comfortably in Hungary (approx. HUF 250,000/month) without immediate employment.

embassy_submission_docs: Valid Passport (Valid for 3+ months beyond intended stay)
Appendix 14: Residence Permit for the Purpose of Studies (D-visa form)
Official Acceptance Letter (Letter of Admission) from the University
Biometric Photo (35mm x 45mm, White background)
Proof of Accommodation (Dormitory certificate or notarized lease agreement)
Proof of Financial Means (Bank statements or Scholarship award letter)
coe_phase_documents: Academic Degrees & Transcripts (Translated into English/Hungarian)
Police Clearance Certificate (Apostilled/Legalized by MFA)
Comprehensive Health Insurance (Minimum €30,000 coverage)
Flight Reservation (One-way or Round-trip)
Proof of Tuition Fee Payment (Bank transfer receipt)
Birth Certificate (Legalized for future local registration)

processing_and_approval_metrics.coe_processing_time: 2 - 4 weeks (University evaluation and admission letter issuance)
processing_and_approval_metrics.visa_processing_time: 15 - 30 days (Processing via the National Directorate-General for Aliens Policing - NDGAP)
processing_and_approval_metrics.approval_chances: High for students with authenticated documents and verified scholarship status.
processing_and_approval_metrics.interview_probability: High. Consular officers often conduct an interview to verify the student's motivation and financial stability.

high_risk_reasons: Insufficient bank balance relative to the cost of living and tuition.
Providing an unconfirmed or informal housing arrangement (e.g., just a hotel booking).
Academic documents that are not legalized or lack the proper translation.
Inability to explain the study plan or why the specific Hungarian university was chosen.
Previous Schengen visa rejections that were not disclosed in the application.
rejection_risk_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Ensure your housing proof includes the 'Title Deed' (Tulajdoni lap) of the property if staying in private rental accommodation.

work_rights_and_stay.part_time_permission: Up to 30 hours per week during the semester (Updated for 2026).
work_rights_and_stay.vacation_hours: Full-time work allowed (up to 90 days or 66 working days per year) during breaks.
work_rights_and_stay.visa_extension: Must apply for the physical Residence Permit card at the local immigration office within 30 days of arrival.
work_rights_and_stay.post_study_work: Eligible for a 9-month 'Study-to-Work' Residence Permit to find a job or start a business after graduation.

step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_1: Apply to a Hungarian university or via the Stipendium Hungaricum portal and receive your 'Letter of Admission'.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_2: Pay the tuition fee deposit (if self-funded) and secure your accommodation certificate/lease.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_3: Complete the online application via the 'Enter Hungary' portal (if applicable) or book an Embassy appointment.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_4: Attend the visa interview at the Hungarian Consulate or VFS Global with all legalized documents.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_5: Once the D-visa sticker is issued, travel to Hungary within the 30-day validity window.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_6: Visit the local Immigration Office (OIF/NDGAP) within 30 days of arrival to submit biometrics.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_7: Receive your physical Residence Permit card and register your address to get an Address Card (Lakcímkártya).

faq_student_edition.question: Can I travel to other Schengen countries with a Hungarian student visa?
faq_student_edition.answer: Yes. Once you have your physical Residence Permit card, you can travel freely within the Schengen Area for up to 90 days in any 180-day period.

faq_student_edition.question: What is the 'Address Card' and why is it important?
faq_student_edition.answer: The 'Lakcímkártya' is an official document proving your place of residence. It is required for opening a bank account, getting a tax ID, and local health services.

faq_student_edition.question: Do I need to learn Hungarian for the visa?
faq_student_edition.answer: No, if your program is in English. However, Stipendium Hungaricum recipients are usually required to take basic Hungarian language and culture classes.

romania.title: Romania Student Visa & Residence Permit Guide 2026 for International Students
romania.description: A complete roadmap for international students seeking higher education in Romania. Covering the D/SD Long-Stay Visa, Ministry of Education Acceptance Letters, MFA scholarships, financial subsistence requirements, and 2026 work-hour regulations.

visa_category_details.entry_type: Multiple Entry (Initially valid for 90 days, extended to a Residence Permit upon arrival)
visa_category_details.eligibility: Applicants must have a Letter of Acceptance from the Romanian Ministry of Education and be enrolled in an accredited state or private institution for full-time education.

government_funded.name: Romania Government Scholarship (MFA)
government_funded.benefits: Full tuition waiver, free accommodation in student dormitories, and a monthly stipend (approx. €65 - €85 depending on degree level).
government_funded.application_window: Typically mid-January to March 31 annually via the 'Study in Romania' portal.
government_funded.selection_criteria: Non-EU citizens with strong academic records; selection is based on file evaluation by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MFA) and Ministry of Education.

private_and_partial.name: Eugen Ionescu Scholarships
private_and_partial.benefits: Financial support for PhD and postdoctoral research stays (3–6 months) at partner universities.
private_and_partial.target: Students from member countries of the Francophonie (OIF).

private_and_partial.name: CEEPUS Exchange Grants
private_and_partial.benefits: Monthly living allowance and accommodation support for short-term student mobility.
private_and_partial.target: Students from Central and South-Eastern Europe participating in regional exchange networks.

private_and_partial.name: University-Specific Merit Scholarships
private_and_partial.benefits: Partial tuition fee waivers for high-performing self-funded students.
private_and_partial.special_note: Common at major hubs like UBB Cluj, University of Bucharest, and Politehnica Bucharest.

english_language_test_requirements_2026.overview: If the study program is in English, students must provide a recognized certificate. If studying in Romanian, a preparatory year is mandatory unless a B2 certificate is already held.

duolingo_det.acceptance_note: Accepted by many private and technical universities for admission, but check with the specific Faculty first.

toefl_ibt.note: Widely accepted; scores must be sent directly to the university or verified via the ETS portal.

financial_solvency_thresholds.self_funded_min_balance: BDT 1,000,000 - 1,400,000 (Calculated as the minimum gross salary/month × duration of the visa, plus the 1st year tuition fee).
financial_solvency_thresholds.sponsor_profile: Immediate family members. Requires a notarized sponsorship letter (Engagement of Maintenance) and proof of the sponsor's income.
financial_solvency_thresholds.bank_history_requirement: Last 3 months of bank statements. Sudden large deposits are highly scrutinized; account activity must appear logical and stable.
financial_solvency_thresholds.logic: The 'Means of Support' rule requires students to show roughly €500–€700 per month for the duration of the visa to cover living expenses in Romania.

embassy_submission_docs: Valid Passport (at least 6 months validity remaining)
Ministry of Education Letter of Acceptance (Original + Copy)
Proof of Tuition Fee Payment for at least one academic year
Proof of Financial Means (Bank statements/Sponsorship)
Criminal Record Certificate (Police Clearance) legalized/apostilled
Travel Medical Insurance (Minimum €30,000 coverage)
coe_phase_documents: Academic Diplomas & Transcripts (Authorized translation into Romanian/English)
Birth Certificate (Legalized/Apostilled and translated)
Medical Certificate (Stating the applicant does not suffer from contagious diseases)
Visa Application Form (Type D) filled and signed
2 Recent Color Photos (3x4 cm)
Proof of Accommodation (University dorm contract or notarized rental lease)

processing_and_approval_metrics.coe_processing_time: 2 - 6 weeks (Ministry of Education evaluation of academic files)
processing_and_approval_metrics.visa_processing_time: 30 - 45 days (After submitting the file to the Romanian Consulate/Embassy)
processing_and_approval_metrics.approval_chances: High if the Letter of Acceptance is verified and the tuition fee is paid in full.
processing_and_approval_metrics.interview_probability: Medium. Most consulates require a brief interview to verify language skills and study intent.

high_risk_reasons: Incomplete or un-notarized financial documents (e.g., bank statements without a wet signature).
Failure to prove full payment of the first year's tuition fee.
Significant academic gaps that cannot be justified by professional work experience.
Inconsistent information between the visa interview and the submitted study plan.
Medical certificate lacking specific clearance for 'infectious and contagious diseases'.
rejection_risk_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Ensure all academic and civil documents have the correct Hague Apostille or Ministry of Foreign Affairs legalization before translation.

work_rights_and_stay.part_time_permission: Up to 4 hours per day (20 hours per week) without a separate work permit.
work_rights_and_stay.vacation_hours: Increased hours are often possible during semester breaks with university approval.
work_rights_and_stay.visa_extension: Must apply for a Residence Permit (Permis de Ședere) at the General Inspectorate for Immigration (IGI) at least 30 days before the 90-day entry visa expires.
work_rights_and_stay.post_study_work: Eligible for a 9-month extension after graduation to search for a job or start a business (introduced in 2024/2025).

step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_1: Apply for university admission and receive the Ministry of Education 'Letter of Acceptance'.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_2: Pay the full 1st year tuition fee to the university's account and save the transfer receipt.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_3: Gather and legalize/apostille all academic and criminal record documents.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_4: Register on the E-Visa portal (eviza.mae.ro) and upload your digital dossier.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_5: Attend the in-person appointment at the Romanian Embassy for biometrics and document submission.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_6: Upon arrival in Romania, visit the IGI office within the first 60 days to apply for your Residence Permit card.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_7: Undergo a local medical check-up in Romania if required for the residence permit finalization.

faq_student_edition.question: Is Romania part of the Schengen Area for students?
faq_student_edition.answer: As of 2024/2025, Romania is partially in Schengen (air/sea). A Romanian student residence permit allows travel to other Schengen countries for 90 days in any 180-day period.

faq_student_edition.question: Can I study in Romania for free?
faq_student_edition.answer: Only if you win the MFA or Ministry of Education scholarship. Otherwise, non-EU students must pay tuition fees, which are among the lowest in the EU.

faq_student_edition.question: Do I need to speak Romanian to live there?
faq_student_edition.answer: In major cities like Bucharest or Cluj, English is widely spoken. However, basic Romanian is helpful for administrative tasks at the Immigration office.

cyprus.title: Cyprus Student Visa & Residency Guide for International Students
cyprus.description: A complete roadmap for international students seeking higher education in Cyprus. Covering the Entry Permit process, the 'Pink Slip' (Temporary Residence Permit), university scholarships, English proficiency, mandatory medical screenings, and work rights in specific sectors.

visa_category_details.visa_type: Entry Permit & Temporary Residence Permit (Pink Slip)
visa_category_details.entry_type: Single Entry Permit (to enter), converted to a Multiple Entry Residence Card after arrival
visa_category_details.eligibility: Applicants must be enrolled in a full-time program at a recognized Cypriot college or university and have their documents cleared by the Migration Department.

government_funded.name: Cyprus Government Scholarship (State Scholarship Foundation)
government_funded.benefits: Varies; can include partial tuition coverage and monthly stipends for excellence.
government_funded.application_window: Varies by year; usually announced through the Ministry of Education and Culture.
government_funded.selection_criteria: Based strictly on academic merit and the applicant's country of origin (bilateral agreements).

private_and_partial.name: International Student Scholarship (Undergraduate)
private_and_partial.benefits: Automatic 50% tuition fee reduction for most international undergraduate students.
private_and_partial.target: All new international students at many private universities (e.g., CIU, EUC).

private_and_partial.name: Academic Excellence / Merit Scholarship
private_and_partial.benefits: 75% to 100% tuition fee waivers.
private_and_partial.target: High-achieving students with a CGPA above 3.50 or outstanding high school grades.

private_and_partial.name: Athletic & Sibling Scholarships
private_and_partial.benefits: 10% to 25% additional discounts on tuition.
private_and_partial.special_note: Offered to students participating in university sports teams or for multiple family members enrolled at the same time.

english_language_test_requirements.overview: Cypriot institutions follow the CEFR framework. While many offer an internal foundation year, direct entry into degree programs requires standardized test scores.

duolingo_det.acceptance_note: Accepted by several private universities for admission and placement into the English prep school.

toefl_ibt.note: Widely accepted; scores must be verifiable via the ETS portal or a certified copy.

financial_solvency_thresholds.self_funded_min_balance: BDT 1,400,000 - 1,800,000 (Equivalent to €12,000 - €15,000 covering tuition, living expenses, and a return ticket).
financial_solvency_thresholds.sponsor_profile: Immediate family. Must provide an 'Affidavit of Support' and a bank letter confirming the ability to fund the entire duration of study.
financial_solvency_thresholds.bank_history_requirement: 6 months of stable history. Statements must be original, stamped, and signed by the bank manager.
financial_solvency_thresholds.logic: The Migration Department requires proof of at least €7,000 - €9,000 for living costs alone, plus the total annual tuition fee.

embassy_submission_docs: Valid Passport (at least 2 years validity remaining)
Original Acceptance Letter from the Cypriot institution
Police Clearance Certificate (Apostilled, valid for 6 months)
Original Bank Letter & Statements (last 6 months)
HIV, Hepatitis B/C, and Syphilis Test Results (Originals)
Chest X-Ray Report for Tuberculosis (Original)
coe_phase_documents: Academic Certificates (Apostilled/Legalized by MFA)
Tuition Fee Receipt (Minimum payment of €3,000 often required)
Non-Refundable Application Fee Receipt (approx. €100 - €150)
Health Insurance Policy (Valid in Cyprus)
Cash in hand of at least €2,000 for arrival personal expenses
MHE (Ministry of Higher Education) approval/stamps where applicable

processing_and_approval_metrics.coe_processing_time: 2 - 4 weeks (University screening and Ministry of Education approval)
processing_and_approval_metrics.visa_processing_time: 4 - 8 weeks (Migration Department review and Entry Permit issuance)
processing_and_approval_metrics.approval_chances: Good if medicals are clear and documents are apostilled; high scrutiny on bank statement stability.
processing_and_approval_metrics.interview_probability: High. The Cyprus Consulate often conducts a personal interview to verify the student's background and intentions.

high_risk_reasons: Positive medical results for Hepatitis, HIV, or Tuberculosis.
Providing bank statements that are older than 3 months or have irregular deposits.
Passport validity less than 18-24 months.
Inability to present original apostilled academic documents at the airport or embassy.
Failing to prove that the first year's tuition has been partially or fully paid.
rejection_risk_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Double-check the apostille requirements for your specific country of residence and ensure medical tests are within the 4-month validity window.

work_rights_and_stay.part_time_permission: Up to 20 hours per week (Allowed ONLY after the first 6 months/1st semester).
work_rights_and_stay.vacation_hours: Full-time work allowed (38 hours per week) during official university breaks.
work_rights_and_stay.visa_extension: Must apply for the Temporary Residence Permit (Pink Slip) within 7 days of arrival in Cyprus.
work_rights_and_stay.post_study_work: Eligible for a 6 to 12-month extension for job-seeking purposes depending on the degree and sector.

step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_1: Apply to a Cypriot university and receive your 'Letter of Acceptance'.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_2: Pay the initial tuition fee and application fee as requested by the university.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_3: Undergo medical tests and gather police clearance; have all documents apostilled/legalized.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_4: The university submits your file to the Migration Department in Cyprus for an Entry Permit.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_5: Once the Entry Permit is issued, book an appointment at the Cyprus Embassy for the visa sticker.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_6: Travel to Cyprus and undergo a second medical check-up at a local government lab.
step_by_step_application_roadmap.step_7: Submit your passport and biometrics to the Migration office for your physical Residence Card (Pink Slip).

faq_student_edition.question: Can I work immediately after arriving in Cyprus?
faq_student_edition.answer: No. International students must wait until they have completed their first academic semester before they are legally allowed to work part-time.

faq_student_edition.question: What is the 'Pink Slip'?
faq_student_edition.answer: It is the colloquial name for the Temporary Residence Permit. You must keep this card with you at all times as proof of your legal status in Cyprus.

faq_student_edition.question: Is North Cyprus (TRNC) different from the Republic of Cyprus?
faq_student_edition.answer: Yes. This guide covers the Republic of Cyprus (South). North Cyprus has a separate visa regime and students there cannot freely enter the South or other Schengen countries.
